storyHook: 到车站买票上车，到站下车找路

narrative.when: 工作日早上八点，要赶去公司开会
narrative.where: 家附近公交站台 → 市中心站
narrative.who: 甲（乘客）· 乙（公交站务员/司机）
narrative.how: 看站牌、排队上车、刷卡投币
narrative.method: 问哪路车、确认方向、到站前按铃下车
narrative.event: 甲第一次走这条路线，怕坐错车迟到

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Which car goes to the middle of town?
turns.zh: 请问，哪路马车到市中心？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Take the number 3. It goes to the east station.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Number 3. Right. And I get on here?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, this stop. Keep on this side.
turns.zh: 对，就这个站台。在这边等。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: How long does it take to get there?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: About twenty minutes, if the roads are clear.
turns.zh: 不堵车的话，二十分钟左右。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I have a meeting at nine. Will I be on time?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is enough time. The car comes every ten minutes.
turns.zh: 时间够。这路车十分钟一班。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. I will be here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Keep your card ready. The man is quick.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Here comes a car. Is that the number 3?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, that is it. Get in now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right. I will go to the door now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Watch your step. The man will have a look at your card.
turns.zh: 小心脚下。司机会看你的卡。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thanks. I hope the card works this time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The card is good. I saw the green light.
turns.zh: 卡没问题。我看见绿灯亮了。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. I will take that seat by the window.
turns.zh: 好。我坐那个靠窗的位置。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The car is moving now. The streets look new to me.
turns.zh: 车开了。这街景我都不认识。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: You are going to the middle of the town, yes?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes. I have a meeting there at nine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Then you have time. It is only twenty minutes away.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I see the names on the board, but I am not certain.
turns.zh: 我看了板子上的站名，但心里没底。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Look at the bell over your head. Give it a push before your stop.
turns.zh: 看你头顶的铃。到站前推一下。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. I will keep my hand near it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: The middle of the town is our stop. Get ready.
turns.zh: 下一站是市中心。准备下车。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I see that tall building. Is that the stop?
turns.zh: 我看到那栋高楼了。是这站吗？

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, that is it. Push the bell now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I will. Thanks for your help.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: No trouble. I go this way every day.
turns.zh: 没事。我每天都走这条路。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The car is slow now. We are near.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes. Get your bag ready.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I have it. I will get off first.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. The door will open on the right.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. I see the door light is on.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Off you go. Have a good day.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Thanks. Which way now?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Go straight. Two hundred steps. See the tall building?
turns.zh: 直走。两百步。看到那栋高楼了吗？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, the one with the blue glass.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That's it. Your office is on the third floor.
turns.zh: 就是那栋。你公司在三楼。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Great. I made it on time. Thanks again.
turns.zh: 太好了。我准时到了。再次感谢。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right. I will go in now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Have you got a meeting card?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, I have it here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Take it to the front. They will give you a card.
turns.zh: 拿到前台去，他们会给你一张卡。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. I will do that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The lift is on the left. Take it to the third floor.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thanks. I see the lift now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is all right. I have to go back to the car.
turns.zh: 没关系。我得回车上去了。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That is kind of you. Thanks.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is all right. Good will with your meeting.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: The car is moving now. I hope I am on the right one.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: You are on the right one. I saw you get on 3.
turns.zh: 没错，我瞧见你上的是3路。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That is a help. I am new to this road.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: No need to be troubled. I will say when we get near.
turns.zh: 别担心。到站前我会提醒你。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thank you. How many stops to the town middle?
turns.zh: 谢谢。到市中心还有几站？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Five more. After that is the market.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I will keep an eye on the glass then.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: You may do that. Or push the bell when you see the town house.
turns.zh: 也行。或者看到市政大楼就按铃。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good idea. I will do that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The town house has a clock. You will see it.
turns.zh: 市政厅有个钟，你会看到的。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I see the clock now. Is this the stop?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, this is it. Push the bell.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I will. Thank you for your help.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: No trouble. Have a good day at work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thanks. I hope I am not late.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: You are on time. The clock says ten to nine.
turns.zh: 来得及。钟显示八点五十。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That is good. The door is opening.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Step down with care. The road is wet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I will. sure and thanks again.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: sure. Keep to the left side.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Which way is the office from here?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Turn left at the road meeting. It is the tall building.
turns.zh: 路口左转，就是那栋高楼。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I see it. That is the one with glass walls.
turns.zh: 看见了，就是那个玻璃墙的。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes. You will be there in two minutes.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right. I will go quick.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Do not run. The floor is wet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right. I will take my time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is wise. Have a good meeting.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thank you. You are very kind.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: No trouble. Off you go now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I am at the door now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Good. Push the door, it is not locked.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: It is open. I am inside.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The meeting room is on the first floor, second door on the right.
turns.zh: 会议室在一楼，右手第二间。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I see a sign. Room 102.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is the one. Go in and take a seat.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Some are in there now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: They are early. You are on time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I will take a seat at the back.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Good idea. The front is for the head man.
turns.zh: 好主意，前面是老板的位子。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: The head man is not here now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: He will be here quick. He is not late.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I hope the talk is short. I have much to do.
turns.zh: 希望会议简短，我还有很多事。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: It will be quick. Only news on the new work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. I did my part last night.
turns.zh: 那就好，我昨晚准备好了。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Then you have nothing to fear. Just say what you did.
turns.zh: 那你没什么好怕的，照实说就行。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right. I will keep it simple.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The meeting is done. You did well.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thanks. I was on edge, but it went well.
turns.zh: 谢谢，我本来紧张，但挺顺利的。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: First time on this line, and you made it.
turns.zh: 第一次坐这条线，你也赶到了。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes. I will take this line another time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Now you see the way. No more fear.
turns.zh: 现在你认路了，不用怕了。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: True. But I still keep my hand near the bell.
turns.zh: 没错，不过我还是会手放铃边。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is a good way for a person who goes by car.
turns.zh: 这对任何坐马车的人来说都是个好习惯。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Let us get a drink before you go back?
turns.zh: 你回去前要不要喝杯东西？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I would like that. A cup of tea will be good.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: There is a place near here. Come on.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. But I have only ten minutes before my train comes.
turns.zh: 行，不过十分钟后车就来了，我只能待一小会儿。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: That is enough for a quick cup. They do take-away here.
turns.zh: 那够喝杯快的，这家店有外带杯。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. One tea, no sugar, and I am off again.
turns.zh: 好的。一杯茶，不加糖，喝完我就走。

storyBlurb: 新人甲第一次坐公交去市中心开会，怕坐错车迟到。在站台问路、刷卡上车、紧盯站牌，最终顺利抵达。
storyScript:
【开场·到达车站】早上八点，甲站在家附近的公交站台，看着陌生的站牌，手心微微出汗。他要去市中心开会，这是第一次走这条路线。站台上人不多，他看见一位穿制服的乙在值班。甲快步上前，指着站牌上模糊的线路图，问：哪路车能到市中心？乙抬头看了一眼，说：坐3路，方向是东站，别坐反了。甲连声道谢，心里默念着车号。

【进行·上车验票】几分钟后，3路车缓缓进站。甲跟着几个人排队上车，车门打开，司机乙坐在驾驶座旁。甲掏出公交卡，在刷卡机上滴了一声，但机器没反应。司机乙提醒：卡要贴紧。甲重新刷了一次，听到“叮”的一声，才松了口气。他找了个靠窗的位置坐下，把包放在腿上，眼睛盯着车内的电子屏。

【进行·途中确认】车开了，甲看着窗外陌生的街景，心里有些忐忑。他每隔一会儿就抬头看电子屏上的站名，生怕坐过。旁边一位老人看他紧张，主动说：小伙子，去市中心吧？还有五站。甲点点头，老人笑着指指头顶的按钮：到站前按铃，司机会停。甲感激地笑了笑，手一直放在铃边。

【收束·下车抵达】当电子屏显示“市中心站”时，甲赶紧按铃。车停稳后，他快步下车，站在陌生的街道上。掏出手机导航，确认公司就在前方两百米。他深吸一口气，迈开步子，嘴角微微上扬。第一次坐公交，虽然紧张，但总算顺利到达。
